pattern: The largest qualifying palindrome is found by generating candidate palindromes from their upper half in descending order and accepting the first one that factors into two numbers of the required digit count, then reducing modulo 1337.
hints: Searching every pair of n-digit numbers is wasteful; instead search the palindromes themselves, from largest downward, and stop at the first that works.
A palindrome with an even number of digits is fully determined by its first half — pick the half, mirror it, and you have the candidate; iterate the half from its maximum downward.
For each candidate palindrome, test whether it has a divisor in the n-digit range whose cofactor is also in that range; the first candidate that does is the answer, which you then take modulo 1337.

edge_inputs.description: single-digit case where the answer is just the largest one-digit product palindrome
two-digit case from the statement, 99*91=9009 -> 9009 mod 1337
the largest n the brute-force oracle can confirm in time; the modulo is applied after finding the true maximum, not to intermediate products

stress.description: every n in the brute-checkable range, exercised repeatedly
stress.note: n is bounded to 5 so the independent double-loop oracle stays fast; the optimal solution itself handles the full constraint range up to 8
